notes: Templated EMS PCR documentation checklists by run type. Specific agency documentation requirements govern.

runTypes.name: Cardiac arrest
items: All shocks delivered with energy and time
All medications given with dose, route, time
Airway interventions (BVM, supraglottic, intubation) with time
Hospital notification time and receiving facility

runTypes.name: Motor vehicle accident
items: Mechanism (speed, direction of impact, restraints, airbag deployment, ejection, intrusion)
Vehicle damage and patient compartment intrusion
Initial GCS and changes en route
Vital signs on scene and at intervals
Spinal motion restriction decision and rationale
Visible injuries with location and characteristics
Pain assessment with scale and any analgesics given
Trauma triage step met (per CDC field triage if applicable)

runTypes.name: Stroke
items: Stroke center notification time and ETA
Family contact information for consent and history

runTypes.name: Overdose
items: Initial mental status and respiratory effort
Naloxone doses given with dose, route, time
Response to naloxone (return of respirations, mental status)
Vital signs on scene and en route
Disposition (transport vs refusal) and capacity assessment if refusal

runTypes.name: Fall
items: Mobility before and after the event

runTypes.name: Syncope
items: Vital signs supine and standing if safe
Family history of sudden cardiac death

runTypes.name: Seizure
items: Vital signs on scene and en route
Benzodiazepine doses given with route and time

runTypes.name: Respiratory distress
items: Initial SpO2 on room air and on supplemental O2
Treatments given (albuterol, ipratropium, nebulizer, NIPPV) with response
Position of comfort and need for advanced airway

runTypes.name: OB / labor and delivery
items: Membrane status (intact vs ruptured) and time of rupture
Visible crowning and any deliveries en route
APGAR at 1 and 5 minutes if delivered
Maternal vital signs and fetal heart tones if available
